subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, narrowly framed, and consistently linked to the analysis
Topic is communicated accurately and effectively from the introduction through the chapter headings
weaknesses: Methodology lacks explicit justification for source and sub-theme selection
Absence of explicit inclusion criteria and minimal source evaluation weakens methodological transparency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates strong focus through clear topic communication and a well-framed research question, placing it solidly in the 3â4 band. However, the methodology is only âmostly complete,â with insufficient explanation of source selection and thematic lenses. These strengths and weaknesses combine to warrant a score of 4 out of 6.

strengths: Primary quotations are judiciously chosen and generally support the key thematic arguments
Terminology and concepts are used correctly in most cases, demonstrating a clear grasp of literary analysis
weaknesses: Reliance on popular websites (e.g. Study.com) and a broken link undermine the academic rigor of secondary sources
Occasional factual errors and inconsistent terminology highlight gaps in subject knowledge and source integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The application of source material and subject knowledge is generally clear, but the frequent use of weaker secondary sources, broken links, and some factual inaccuracies limit the work to the mid-low range of the 3â4 band. A score of 3 out of 6 reflects appropriately selective strength and notable areas for improvement.

strengths: Research is purposeful and largely relevant to the research question, drawing on a broad bibliography
Structure of analysis supports coherent thematic comparisons across characters
weaknesses: Analysis remains at a descriptive level in places and lacks depth of theoretical engagement
Discussion and evaluation contain inconsistencies and miss opportunities for counter-argument and source critique
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the research quality is good and thematic comparisons are coherent, the depth of analysis and evaluation is only adequate, with limited critique of secondary views. These factors position the work in the 4â6 band but toward its lower end, justifying a score of 4 out of 12.

strengths: Structure is clearly appropriate with a complete table of contents and logical chapter headings
Layout elements (margins, font size, page numbering) are consistently applied
weaknesses: Citation styles vary between APA and MLA and one URL remains broken
Minor pagination mismatches and redundant material in the conclusion detract from overall polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay meets all basic presentation requirements, earning it a place in the 1â2 band. However, inconsistencies in citation formatting, a broken link, and stylistic lapses limit the work to a score of 2 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is **prominently stated, narrowly framed** around the three women, and revisited in topic sentences and the conclusion.  The formulation is specific enough to steer discussion (female perspectives, achievement of individual dreams) and is applied consistently throughout the thematic chapters.  Connection between the question and the analysis is therefore effective and sustained, justifying placement in the top band for this strand.

The essay offers thematic comparison and some interpretive insight (e.g., Myrtleâs jealousy as social constraint), yet much commentary remains **descriptive** and theoretical frameworks are under-used.  Conclusions usually follow but can be superficial.  This reflects âadequateâ analysis with partial relevance, corresponding to the mid-band (4â6).

Primary quotations are well chosen and generally linked to the sub-themes, and most secondary sources are relevant.  Nonetheless, a broken link, reliance on Study.com, and limited integration of economic papers reduce full appropriateness.  Overall application is **mostly relevant and appropriate** rather than consistently so, matching the 3â4 descriptor.

Margins, font size and page numbers are consistent, and the word count plus title appear correctly.  In-text citation style fluctuates between APA and MLA and one URL is broken, yet all mandatory layout elements are present and correctly applied overall, justifying the top layout band (3â4).

The student shows **clear knowledge of plot, context and character motivations**, referencing 1920s social divisions and feminist angles.  Occasional factual errors (e.g., mis-dating the Declaration of Independence) and moments of superficial summary prevent a stronger band, but the knowledge remains coherent and largely supported by sources, fitting the 3â4 range.

The EE follows IB conventions with title page, abstract substitute, contents, chapters and references.  Headings guide the reader logically.  Minor pagination mismatches do not undermine overall suitability, so the structure is **clearly appropriate and complete**, meeting the 3â4 band.

The bibliography is broad, drawing on primary text, academic journals and reputable reference works, and most items link directly to the research question.  While a few popular sources and one dead link exist, overall **research is purposeful and clearly relevant**, warranting the 7â9 bandâbut not the top (10â12) because source critique is limited.

The student communicates the topic **clearly and consistently** from the title page onward, reiterating the intention to examine the American Dream through three female characters.  The introduction lays out purpose, scope and structure in an accessible way, and each chapter heading explicitly links back to the overarching theme.  Minor wording slips (e.g. âpolythreeâ) do not impede understanding, so the work meets the descriptor for accurate and effective communication with an appropriate focus.

A coherent discussion structure is present and the conclusion revisits the research question, but counter-arguments are brief and evaluation of secondary views is limited.  Reasoning is therefore **adequate but contains inconsistencies**, matching the 4â6 descriptor rather than the higher bands that require fully focused, coherent argument.

A basic method is outlined (compare four sub-themes across three characters) and primary/secondary sources are listed, indicating **some informed selection**.  However, the essay never explains *why* the chosen sub-themes or secondary pieces were selected, nor does it justify the absence of other lenses.  Source evaluation is minimal and the criteria for inclusion are implicit rather than explicit, so the methodology is only âmostly complete,â aligning with the 3â4 band.

Key conceptsââsocial mobility,â âsymbolism,â âgender rolesââare used **correctly in most cases**, and quotations are integrated with suitable literary terms.  Precision lapses (âcarrierâ for âcareerâ, âpolythreeâ) show inconsistency, so terminology is âmostly accurateâ rather than fully consistent, placing the work in the 3â4 band.

annotations.comment: Jordan Bakerâs autonomy is well exemplified, but the absence of source justification (e.g., choice of Murtoff, 2024) weakens methodological transparency.
Your interpretation of Daisyâs conformity is insightful but would be strengthened by contrasting with secondary critiques to deepen the evaluation of her agency.
The conclusion restates key findings but largely repeats earlier content; condense redundant material and emphasize novel analytical insights.
The citation of the Declaration of Independence as 1911 is incorrect; verify original publication dates and correct the reference to demonstrate subject knowledge.
Your use of Daisyâs home quote effectively illustrates status, but analyzing the spatial symbolism through a feminist lens would deepen critical insight.
Myrtleâs living conditions are vividly described with a well-chosen quotation, demonstrating strong subject knowledge of socio-economic symbolism.
The concluding synthesis of education impacts is concise, but critically reflect on the reliability and scope of your secondary sources (e.g., Yuqing & Jianling, 2021).
While summarizing Daisyâs outcomes, the conclusion omits methodological limitations such as selection bias in your source material; include a brief critique.
The reference list is comprehensive but citation styles vary between APA and MLA; standardize formatting to ensure consistency and professionalism.
Reliance on Study.com as a secondary source weakens academic rigor; prioritize peer-reviewed journals to strengthen the credibility of your analysis.
The title page clearly states the investigationâs title and research focus, immediately orienting the reader to the topic and context.
The title is descriptive but could be sharpened by specifying the character focus and thematic lens to better define the scope and avoid ambiguity.
The research question is prominently stated and narrowly framed around female perspectives, effectively guiding the analysis throughout the essay.
Consider aligning page numbers with dot leaders and ensuring consistent entry formatting to enhance the professional presentation of the contents list.
The word count is correctly provided and remains within the 4,000-word limit, satisfying the IB extended essay formatting requirement.
The table of contents is complete and logically organized, facilitating easy navigation through the chapters and subsections.
The introduction effectively communicates the novelâs context and research purpose, establishing a clear focus on the three female characters.
The methodology outlines comparative sub-themes but lacks explicit justification for selecting these specific dimensions; stating inclusion criteria would enhance completeness.
Application of historical context is relevant, but critically evaluating secondary sources like popular websites would strengthen the appropriateness of source material.
Introducing four sub-concepts is useful, but explicitly linking each to individual character analyses will enhance the coherent application of key terminology.
The discussion of gender inequality is descriptive but lacks depth; applying a feminist theoretical framework could deepen the critical evaluation of character agency.
While the overview of social classes is accurate, the essay should explain why these divisions matter for the female charactersâ pursuit of the American Dream to clarify methodological reasoning.
Restating the research question with named characters reinforces its connection to the upcoming analysis, sustaining focus and clarity throughout the essay.
When transitioning to the homeownership theme, explicitly link stability back to each characterâs American Dream to maintain clear thematic coherence.
Your discussion of Daisyâs education lacks critical engagement with how 1920s gender norms restricted womenâs educational opportunities; deepen the analysis with scholarly sources.
This section defines a useful thematic lens but employs unclear terms like âpolythreeâ; standardize terminology for consistent conceptual understanding.
The quotation from Fitzgerald is judiciously chosen and directly supports your argument about Daisyâs motivations, demonstrating strong primary source application.
The analysis of Myrtleâs moral complexity is underdeveloped; integrating critical perspectives on class and gender dynamics would enrich this discussion.
The golf champion quotation is well-integrated and convincingly evidences Jordanâs upward mobility, demonstrating effective primary source use.
The risks of Myrtleâs double life are noted, but discussing the narrativeâs moral perspective and integrating critical sources would enhance your analysis.
The synthesis of this sub-theme is clear but overgeneralizes; refine to highlight nuanced differences in each characterâs outcome relative to your research question.
The opening of the education section is a relevant lens, yet the initial sentences are convoluted; streamline language for clarity and academic tone.
The economic prosperity section is clearly labeled, maintaining structural coherence across thematic chapters.
The comparative analysis of economic mobility is coherent, but incorporating historical economic data or scholarly debate would add empirical depth.
The URL for Bruno (2019) is broken; update or replace with an archived or DOI link to ensure source accessibility and reliability.
Including a dedicated reflection section on research challenges and decision-making would demonstrate deeper personal engagement and metacognitive evaluation.
